rulings.Case Title: FISH TATE VS. ISMAEL RUIZ ET AL
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for September 02, 2020, line 2. DEFENDANT FELIX HERNANDEZ, IRASEMA GUTIERREZ MOTION TO STRIKE Due to COVID-19 pandemic telephonic appearances are encouraged. If you appear in-person (a good faith reason for personal appearance is required) for your case notify the department by 5:00 p.m. one day before the hearing at Department501ContestTR@sftc.org. Department will follow social distancing guidelines and you will be required to wear a mask or a face covering. By 8:00 a.m. on the date of the hearing moving and opposing parties to submit electronic copies of their respective proposed orders (note: prevailing party to prepare an order repeating the tentative ruling verbatim in addition to any other proposed orders) in an editable format to Dept501Orders@sftc.org, so that the judge can modify it prior to signing, if needed. All communications shall include in the subject line the case name, number and hearing date. (e.g. "Smith v. Jones; CGC-18-555555; June 1, 2020"). All communications sent to the Court must be copied to the opposing party/counsel for whom e-mail addresses are known. The e-mail address provided herein shall only be used for the purposes indicated above. No unauthorized communications will be considered or responded to. Defendant's Amended Motion to Strike is GRANTED as specified below with leave to amend for Plaintiff to allege facts supporting entitlement to attorney's fees under the Tort of Another Doctrine. cursory allegations contained in paragraph 12 is not sufficient to support such claim, especially as it appears to apply to all causes of action alleged in the Complaint. The Court further notes that the Notice of Motion seeks to strike the entirety of paragraph 4 of the Prayer for Relief. This prayer request costs, inter alia. Request to strike prayer for costs is not supported by points and authorities. Therefore, the motion is granted only as to the request for fees, not as to the request for costs. =(501/CFH)

rulings.Case Title: FISH TATE VS. ISMAEL RUIZ ET AL
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for September 02, 2020, line 2. DEFENDANT FELIX HERNANDEZ, IRASEMA GUTIERREZ Amended DEMURRER to COMPLAINT Due to COVID-19 pandemic telephonic appearances are encouraged. If you appear in-person (a good faith reason for personal appearance is required) for your case notify the department by 5:00 p.m. one day before the hearing at Department501ContestTR@sftc.org. Department will follow social distancing guidelines and you will be required to wear a mask or a face covering. By 8:00 a.m. on the date of the hearing moving and opposing parties to submit electronic copies of their respective proposed orders (note: prevailing party to prepare an order repeating the tentative ruling verbatim in addition to any other proposed orders) in an editable format to Dept501Orders@sftc.org, so that the judge can modify it prior to signing, if needed. All communications shall include in the subject line the case name, number and hearing date. (e.g. "Smith v. Jones; CGC-18-555555; June 1, 2020"). All communications sent to the Court must be copied to the opposing party/counsel for whom e-mail addresses are known. The e-mail address provided herein shall only be used for the purposes indicated above. No unauthorized communications will be considered or responded to. Defendant's Amended Demurer to Complaint is SUSTAINED without leave to amend. Plaintiff has remedies at law available to resolve the matter, e.g. cause of action for ejectment which would necessarily involve the determination of "whether Defendant's Rent Board Rule 12.19 rights have been terminated pursuant to the Notice or law." =(501/CFH)
